claim: Each docs/gen/tools/<command>.md shall be a pure function of that tool's methodology.Tool registry entry (Command/Canon/Purpose/Status), generated wholesale by BuildToolDocs with no hand-written content.
why: A tool doc that diverges from the tool's real declaration is worse than no doc -- it misleads operators. Each tool doc is generated by generator.BuildToolDocs() (internal/generator/tooldocs.go): for every tool in methodology.Tools it renders a single document from the registry fields only -- a status-aware H1 (statusBadge: [IMPLEMENTED]/[PLANNED -- not implemented]), a Status section (statusLine), the Canon section token, and the Purpose line -- and writes it to domains/<domain>/docs/gen/tools/<command>.md via gen-spec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go). The whole file is regenerated wholesale on each gen-spec run (a single AUTOGENERATED banner at the top), so there are no inner sentinels and the doc literally cannot diverge from the registry entry that defines the tool. (Historical note: the original mechanism derived two sections from the module docstring + argparse --help output between hand-edit sentinels; that AST-harvest does not apply here.) The single-source discipline is preserved and strengthened. TestBuildToolDocsSemanticCompleteness (internal/generator/thinkingdocs_tooldocs_test.go) verifies every methodology.Tools entry yields a non-empty doc containing its Canon, Purpose, and Command.
